name: Continuity
domain: Conservation of flow, smooth functions, gauge continuity, unbroken streams
core_insight: What flows in must flow out; what has no source cannot accumulate. Continuity is the mathematical expression of conservation at every point in space. Gauge theories require smooth (differentiable) field configurations — discontinuities are singular and physically forbidden.

key_equations.name: Continuity Equation
key_equations.why: Local conservation: density changes only when flow diverges or converges — no teleportation of substance

key_equations.name: Kirchhoff's Current Law
key_equations.why: Current cannot accumulate at a node: the circuit form of continuity

key_equations.name: Cauchy Continuity
key_equations.why: Mathematical continuity: the function matches its limit — no jumps, no gaps

seed_prompt: ⎯ Trace the flow. Where does it enter? Where does it leave? The difference is accumulation.
